message:
refactor(process_abi): pack posix_spawn_impl args into SpawnRequest struct

The internal posix_spawn_impl helper had grown to 8 parameters
(pid, path, file_actions, attrp, argv, envp, search_path,
pidfd_out). Three public entry points (posix_spawn, posix_spawnp,
pidfd_spawn_impl) call it, each with slightly different defaults
for `pid`, `search_path`, and `pidfd_out`. Passing those as
positional args invites subtle mismatch bugs — e.g. someone swaps
`search_path` and `pidfd_out` order and nothing type-checks
(bool vs *mut c_int are distinct, but future additions of same-type
fields would not be).

This change:

- Introduces `struct SpawnRequest` that owns the 8 previously
  loose parameters. The struct derives nothing and lives in the
  module-private namespace — it's not exposed via any `pub` item.
- Rewrites `posix_spawn_impl` to take a single `request:
  SpawnRequest`, destructure it once at the top (`let SpawnRequest
  { pid, path, .. } = request;`), and use the named fields for the
  rest of the function. Body is otherwise identical.
- Updates all three callers (`posix_spawn`, `posix_spawnp`,
  `pidfd_spawn_impl`) to construct a `SpawnRequest { ... }` literal
  with named fields. Previously-positional "false / std::ptr::null_mut()"
  default args for `search_path` / `pidfd_out` are now explicitly
  named, which is the whole point — a reader can tell at a glance
  which call is the spawn variant, which is the spawnp variant, and
  which is the pidfd variant.

No behavioural change. ABI is unaffected because the three
`pub unsafe extern "C"` entry points keep their exact signatures;
only the private impl was refactored.

Also touches `tests/process_abi_test.rs`: the `wait_for_pidfd_child`
helper had a `assert!(false, "...")` + unreachable `return;` after
the None arm of a match on `pid_from_fdinfo`. Rewrite as a plain
`panic!("...")` which is what clippy's `assertions_on_constants`
wants; also lets us drop the dead `return;`. Pure cleanup — same
failure mode, same message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
